violations.text: Various foods were observed held in the hot hold units at 130 - 132 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Hot hold unit temperatures were increased. Foods were reheated to above 165 degrees F and returned to hot hold units. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2026

Refrigerator door gasket of 2-door reach-in #2 observed in poor repair, split away from the door. Gasket is to be replaced. Service call has already been made. In addition, 2-door reach-in #1 observed in poor repair and is not currently in use. Service call has already been placed. Equipment must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2026

Refrigerator door gaskets of the cafeteria 3-door reach-in were observed in poor repair, splitting away from the doors. Gaskets are to be properly repaired/replaced. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2024

Refrigerator door gasket of the cafeteria 3-door reach-in observed in poor repair, splitting away from the door. Gasket is to be replaced. Kitchen 2-door reach-in #2 is in poor repair. Unit is not in use. Service has been contacted and has started repair. New Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2024

Refrigerator door gaskets of the 3-door reach-in in the cafeteria (right and left doors) were observed in poor repair, splitting away from the doors. Equipment must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/28/2024

The dishwasher and 3-bay sink are still lacking air gaps. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food/food equipment is placed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/31/2023
Receiving door is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/31/2023
3-door reach-in, located in the cafeteria, has a gasket that has split away from the door. Gasket must be replaced. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/31/2023
Dishwasher and 3-bay sink are lacking air gaps. Air gaps are necessary to prevent back-up of sewage/contaminated water into equipment. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/31/2023

The receiving doors and the doors in the cafeteria are not tight-fitting. Space observed between and at the bottom of the doors. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2023
Can opener is not in good repair. The blade observed to be rusting and pitting and must be replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2023
Refrigerator door gaskets of the 3-door reach-in in the cafeteria observed in poor repair. Gaskets must be replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 09/01/2023
The 3-bay sink and dishwasher are still lacking proper air gaps. Air gaps must be created. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/01/2023
